title: Book of Common Prayer (1662) -- Collects
tradition_notes: The 1662 Book of Common Prayer is the foundational liturgical text of the Church of England, authorised under the Act of Uniformity (1662). Its collects draw on older pre-Reformation sources, the 1549/1552 BCPs of Thomas Cranmer, and earlier Latin sacramentaries. This dataset covers the Sunday and Feast Day collects from the Collects, Epistles, and Gospels section.
source_edition: eskimo.com digitization of the Book of Common Prayer (1662), hosted by Lynda M. Howell
notes: The 1662 BCP is in the public domain in the US. UK Crown copyright exists but does not affect a US-based project. The source has no robots.txt crawl-delay restrictions. Five HTML pages cover Parts I-V of the collects (Advent through Saints' Days).
